note: Each sub-topic in the IB DP Chemistry guide has explicit Standard level and higher level (SL/HL) content and may also have Additional higher level (AHL) content. This file records the scope boundaries extracted from the syllabus content section (p.33-64).

level_definitions.SL_HL: Content assessed at both Standard Level and Higher Level.
level_definitions.AHL: Additional Higher Level content assessed only at Higher Level.
level_definitions.SL_HL_only: Sub-topic has no Additional Higher Level content; all content is SL/HL.

subtopics.title: Introduction to the particulate nature of matter
Electron configurations
subtopics.ahl_scope: Structure 1.3.6 Ionization from convergence limit; Structure 1.3.7 Successive ionization energy

subtopics.title: Counting particles by mass: The mole
The covalent model
subtopics.ahl_scope: Structure 2.2 AHL content including formal charge, VSEPR beyond basic shapes, delocalization, etc.

subtopics.title: The periodic table: Classification of elements
subtopics.ahl_scope: Structure 3.1 AHL content including transition elements, complex ions, etc.

subtopics.title: Functional groups: Classification of organic compounds
subtopics.ahl_scope: Structure 3.2 AHL content including advanced organic nomenclature, spectroscopy, etc.

subtopics.title: How much? The amount of chemical change
How fast? The rate of chemical change
subtopics.ahl_scope: Reactivity 2.2 AHL content including rate mechanisms, Arrhenius, etc.

subtopics.title: How far? The extent of chemical change
subtopics.ahl_scope: Reactivity 2.3 AHL content including buffer calculations, pKa/pKb, etc.

subtopics.title: Proton transfer reactions
subtopics.ahl_scope: Reactivity 3.1 AHL content including Lewis acid-base, buffers, indicators, etc.

subtopics.title: Electron transfer reactions
subtopics.ahl_scope: Reactivity 3.2 AHL content including standard electrode potentials, electrolysis of aqueous solutions, etc.

subtopics.title: Electron-pair sharing reactions
subtopics.ahl_scope: Reactivity 3.4 AHL content including SN1/SN2 mechanisms, electrophilic substitution, coordination complexes, etc.

verification_notes: Boundary markers are explicit in the PDF as 'Standard level and higher level: N hours' and 'Additional higher level: N hours' (or 'Additional higher level: None for ...').
Reactivity 1.4 is entirely AHL (no SL/HL hours).
Builder must use this boundary table to tag each topic_id with scope: SL_HL_only, SL_HL_plus_AHL, or AHL_only.
